mechanism_narrative: A persistently high unemployment rate signals weak labor market conditions and subdued consumer confidence, which translates into lower corporate earnings expectations and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of periods where UNRATE is above its 12-month moving average, or the Rank IC is consistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand, directly impacting corporate revenues and profit margins, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth rate turns negative
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the RSAFS 3-month growth rate turns negative, or the signal fails to predict downturns.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate combined with a high unemployment rate signals aggressive monetary easing in a weak labor market, typically supporting asset prices as the economy recovers from a downturn, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances despite the accommodative monetary and weak labor market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained recovery in housing starts (HOUST) indicates improving consumer confidence and investment in the housing sector, which often precedes broader economic expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where HOUST shows sustained recovery after a downturn, or the correlation is weak.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing and overall economic activity, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window when INDPRO growth is positive, indicating no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power, increases input costs for businesses, and often prompts tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite high inflation, or the signal shows no significant negative correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong job creation (PAYEMS growth) and declining unemployment (UNRATE fall) indicates a robust labor market, boosting consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 0.1% AND UNRATE 3-month average change < -0.1%
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where both PAYEMS and UNRATE signal a strengthening labor market.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth signifies a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a favorable environment for corporate earnings and equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized, or the signal's predictive power is not statistically significant.
